Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker-assisted selection: pyramiding low-SGA alleles at SW29 (GAME9) and SW246 (Solyc10g085230) reduces major SGAs to ~20.9%; useful for reducing anti-nutritional compounds when introgressing wild germplasm
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: A premature stop codon (SNP ch10:64501127) in Solyc10g085230 produces a truncated protein that significantly reduces SGA content (e.g., SlFM1885) in ripe fruit, contributing to domestication of less bitter/anti-nutritional fruits
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay (recombinant protein expressed in E. coli BL21 converted tomatidine to tomatidine-galactose); VIGS (virus-induced gene silencing, from prior study Alseekh et al. 2015); mGWAS; natural allelic variation with nonsense mutation
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Recombinant GST-fusion protein expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3); natural accessions with wild-type vs. premature stop codon allele
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly expressed during ripening stages (break and post-break), distinct from GAME1 cluster which is expressed in immature fruits
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Premature stop codon allele (ch10:64501127) associated with lower SGA content (favorable for reduced bitterness/anti-nutritional compounds)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Nonsense mutation abolishes glycosylation of tomatidine, reducing accumulation of downstream SGA glycosides in ripe fruit
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: mGWAS combined with eQTL and co-expression analysis; previously validated via S. pennellii introgression lines
Genes.Genetic_Population: 442 diverse tomato accessions (PIM, CER, BIG); S. pennellii introgression lines (prior study)
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: ch10:64501127 (nonsense mutation introducing premature stop codon in exon)
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Located within a newly discovered co-expression gene cluster on chr10 consisting of 1 acetyltransferase, 1 cytochrome P450, 1 acyl-CoA dehydrogenase, and 7 UDP-glucosyltransferase genes. This cluster has a ripening-specific expression pattern distinct from the previously known GAME1 cluster (immature fruit expression).
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: LC-MS/MS (broadly targeted metabolomics); in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS detection
Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing (SRP045767, PRJNA353161); RNA-seq (PRJNA396272); LC-MS/MS metabolomics (Mendeley Data: 10.17632/gbz22vb344.1)
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Part of domestication sweep SW246; selected during improvement stage
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Premature stop codon allele significantly reduced SlFM1885 content; pyramiding SW29+SW246 loci reduces three major SGAs to 20.9% of original levels
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Loss-of-function via premature stop codon eliminates glycosyltransferase activity
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: GAME9->activates->SGA pathway genes; Solyc10g085230 is part of a newly identified chr10 gene cluster for SGA biosynthesis
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g085230 encodes a UDP-glycosyltransferase that converts tomatidine to tomatidine-galactose, validated by in vitro enzyme assay. 2. A natural nonsense mutation (premature stop codon at ch10:64501127) significantly reduces SGA content in ripe fruit. 3. The gene is part of a newly identified chr10 co-expression cluster with ripening-specific expression, complementing the known GAME1 cluster. 4. Pyramiding the low-SGA allele at this locus with GAME9 (SW29) reduces three major SGAs to ~20.9%.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative metabolite content (LC-MS/MS, normalized log2 values); DW basis (freeze-dried pericarp)
Genes.Variety: Heinz 1706 (reference); E. coli BL21 for in vitro assay

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Marker-assisted selection for reduced anti-nutritional SGAs; pyramiding with Solyc10g085230 low-SGA allele achieves ~79% reduction in three major SGAs
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: A nonsynonymous SNP (V-A, ch01:84029382) in GAME9 CDS is associated with content alteration of 8 SGAs; the low-SGA allele was progressively selected during domestication and improvement
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association with 8 SGAs; allele frequency tracking across domestication stages (PIM→CER→BIG); located within domestication sweep SW29; pyramiding analysis with SW246
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Transcription factor regulating SGA biosynthesis pathway genes
Genes.Experimental_Materials: Natural accessions across PIM (n=31), CER (n=124), BIG (n=287) groups
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Downregulated in BIG relative to CER, consistent with SGA content reduction during improvement
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: A allele (ch01:84029382, V→A) associated with lower SGA content; frequency 0% PIM, 26.3% CER, 57.3% BIG
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Steroidal glycoalkaloids (SGAs; 8 SGAs associated with GAME9 allele)
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Non-synonymous mutation (V→A) reduces transcriptional activation of SGA pathway, decreasing SGA accumulation
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: mGWAS combined with domestication sweep analysis
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GAME9 was previously reported as an important TF for SGA regulation (Cárdenas et al., 2016). This study provides population-level evidence for its role in SGA domestication through allele frequency shifts. Note: direct knockout/OE validation of GAME9 was not performed in this study; evidence is from mGWAS + sweep analysis + allele frequency tracking.
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Located within domestication sweep SW29; key role in SGA domestication
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Pyramiding low-SGA alleles at GAME9 (SW29) and Solyc10g085230 (SW246) reduces three major SGAs to 20.9%; allele frequency for lower SGA content increased from 0% (PIM) to 26.3% (CER) to 57.3% (BIG)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation of SGA biosynthesis structural genes; nonsynonymous mutation likely reduces regulatory activity
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: GAME9->activates->SGA structural genes (cholesterol to dehydrolycoperoside pathway)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME9 harbors a nonsynonymous SNP (V→A) at ch01:84029382 associated with content changes of 8 SGAs. 2. The low-SGA allele frequency increased progressively from 0% (PIM) to 57.3% (BIG), indicating strong selection during domestication. 3. GAME9 is located within domestication sweep SW29. 4. Pyramiding GAME9 low-SGA allele with Solyc10g085230 allele reduces three major SGAs to ~20.9%.
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative metabolite content (LC-MS/MS, normalized log2 values); DW basis
Genes.Variety: Multiple accessions across PIM, CER, BIG groups

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Selection for pink tomatoes (popular in Asian markets) via SlMYB12 loss-of-function inadvertently modifies >100 metabolites beyond flavonoids; breeders should be aware of pleiotropic metabolic effects; molecular markers linked to SlMYB12 useful for metabolome-assisted breeding
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of SlMYB12 in cv. Moneymaker produces pink fruit phenotype with loss of flavonoids in peel and alteration of 152 metabolites including SGAs, polyamines, polyphenols, and primary metabolites, demonstrating SlMYB12 as a major metabolic hub gene
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout (4 independent homozygous mutant lines with 2 different sgRNAs); transcriptome analysis of mutant peel; mGWAS; eQTL; cross-validation with AtMYB12 ChIP targets
Genes.Developmental_Stage: Turning/orange stage fruit (for transcriptome); ripe fruit (for metabolome)
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; key regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis pathway; also influences polyamines, polyphenols, SGAs, and primary metabolites
Genes.Experimental_Materials: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines M1-M4 in Moneymaker background (2 sgRNAs); 44 pink BIG vs 191 red BIG natural accessions
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly expressed in peel at turning stage
Genes.Favorable_Allele_or_Haplotype: Functional allele (red fruit) maintains flavonoid content; loss-of-function alleles (pink fruit) reduce flavonoids but alter >100 other metabolites
Genes.Final_Nutrient_Product: Flavonoids (including naringenin chalcone); polyamines; polyphenols; steroidal glycoalkaloids; primary metabolites
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Knockout eliminates flavonoid accumulation in peel (transparent peel = pink phenotype); redirects or indirectly alters polyamine, SGA, polyphenol, and primary metabolite pathways
Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously cloned (Adato et al., 2009; Ballester et al., 2010; Lin et al., 2014); validated here by CRISPR/Cas9 and multi-omics
Genes.Genetic_Population: 442 diverse tomato accessions; 44 pink BIG vs 191 red BIG
Genes.Key_Variant_Site: Promoter cis-acting element deletion and several nonsense mutations in CDS (Lin et al., 2014); SNPy (signature SNP used for mGWAS)
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: SlMYB12 is identified as a major hub gene of tomato fruit metabolism. Selection for pink fruit (loss of SlMYB12 function) has unexpectedly broad metabolic consequences beyond flavonoids, including changes in 16 SGAs, 12 polyamines, 5 polyphenols, and 11 primary metabolites. This exemplifies how selection for one trait can have major impacts on seemingly unrelated metabolic traits. Two polyamine biosynthesis genes (Solyc05g041860 and Solyc04g063210) were identified as downstream targets via mGWAS and differential expression in knockout lines.
Genes.Nutrient_Assay_or_Profiling_Method: LC-MS/MS (broadly targeted metabolomics); RNA-seq for transcriptome
Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (PRJNA396272); LC-MS/MS metabolomics; resequencing; eQTL; mGWAS
Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Pink vs red fruit color (transparent vs yellow peel); potential taste differences
Genes.Pathway_Name: Flavonoid biosynthesis; polyamine biosynthesis; steroidal glycoalkaloid biosynthesis; primary metabolism
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: 152 metabolites significantly altered in CRISPR knockout fruit peel (p<0.05); 122 metabolites significantly different between pink and red accessions (p<10^-3); includes 18 flavonoids decreased, 16 SGAs changed, 12 polyamines changed, 5 polyphenols changed, 11 primary metabolites changed
Genes.Reaction_Product_or_Immediate_Output: NA (regulatory; controls expression of flavonoid pathway genes and other metabolic genes)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptional activation of flavonoid pathway genes and other metabolic genes; 869 differentially expressed genes in knockout peel (658 up, 211 down; ≥2-fold, p<0.01); 18/44 known AtMYB12 direct ChIP targets confirmed as SlMYB12 eQTL targets
Genes.Regulatory_Relationships: SlMYB12->activates->flavonoid biosynthesis genes; SlMYB12->regulates->Solyc05g041860 (N-acetyltransferase, polyamine pathway); SlMYB12->regulates->Solyc04g063210 (caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase, polyamine pathway); SNPy->eQTL->69 genes
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of SlMYB12 (4 independent lines) produces pink fruit with loss of flavonoids and alteration of 152 metabolites in peel. 2. Beyond flavonoids, knockout affects 16 SGAs, 12 polyamines, 5 polyphenols, and 11 primary metabolites. 3. mGWAS identifies 56 metabolites associated with SlMYB12 signature SNP; eQTL identifies 69 regulated genes. 4. 18/44 known AtMYB12 direct targets confirmed as SlMYB12 eQTL targets, supporting conserved regulatory network. 5. SlMYB12 is a major metabolic hub gene; breeding for pink tomatoes has unexpectedly broad metabolic consequences.
Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Nutrient_Accumulation: Fruit peel (primary site of flavonoid accumulation); fruit pericarp
Genes.Units_and_Basis: Relative metabolite content (LC-MS/MS, normalized log2 values); DW basis (freeze-dried pericarp/peel)
Genes.Variety: Moneymaker (for CRISPR knockout); 44 pink BIG and 191 red BIG accessions for population comparison

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Illustrates unintended metabolic consequences of breeding for pink tomatoes via SlMYB12 loss-of-function
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: mGWAS identified Solyc05g041860 as associated with polyamine SlFM0516; its expression is significantly altered in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout lines, linking SlMYB12 regulation to polyamine metabolism
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association; differential expression in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout peel tissue
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: N-acetyltransferase; key enzyme of polyamine biosynthesis pathway
Genes.Experimental_Materials: SlMYB12 CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines M1-M4 in Moneymaker
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Differentially expressed in SlMYB12 knockout peel
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Expression altered in SlMYB12 knockout, leading to changed polyamine metabolite levels
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Identified as a downstream target linking SlMYB12 to polyamine metabolism. Evidence is from mGWAS association + differential expression in knockout, but direct enzyme assay was not performed in this study.
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Significantly altered in SlMYB12 knockout peel (shown in Figure 4G)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally regulated by SlMYB12 (directly or indirectly)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS identified Solyc05g041860 (N-acetyltransferase) as associated with polyamine metabolite SlFM0516. 2. Expression significantly altered in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout peel. 3. Provides a link between flavonoid regulation (SlMYB12) and polyamine metabolism.

Genes.Breeding_or_Biofortification_Value: Illustrates unintended metabolic consequences of breeding for pink tomatoes via SlMYB12 loss-of-function
Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect_on_Nutrient: mGWAS identified Solyc04g063210 as associated with polyamine SlFM0756; its expression is significantly altered in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout lines, linking SlMYB12 regulation to polyamine metabolism
Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS association; differential expression in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout peel tissue
Genes.Enzyme_Name_or_Function: Caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase; key enzyme of polyamine biosynthesis pathway
Genes.Experimental_Materials: SlMYB12 CRISPR/Cas9 knockout lines M1-M4 in Moneymaker
Genes.Expression_Pattern: Differentially expressed in SlMYB12 knockout peel
Genes.Flux_or_Direction_Change: Expression altered in SlMYB12 knockout, leading to changed polyamine metabolite levels
Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Identified as a downstream target linking SlMYB12 to polyamine metabolism. Caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase is also involved in phenylpropanoid/lignin pathway. Evidence from mGWAS + differential expression in knockout.
Genes.Quantitative_Nutrient_Change: Significantly altered in SlMYB12 knockout peel (shown in Figure 4G)
Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Transcriptionally regulated by SlMYB12 (directly or indirectly)
Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS identified Solyc04g063210 (caffeoyl-CoA O-methyltransferase) as associated with polyamine metabolite SlFM0756. 2. Expression significantly altered in SlMYB12 CRISPR knockout peel. 3. Provides a link between flavonoid regulation (SlMYB12) and polyamine metabolism.

Title: Rewiring of the Fruit Metabolome in Tomato Breeding
